purpose: Planning artifact for Valgaron character category relationships, category-specific field profiles, and relationship-backed character fields.

scope.sourceCategorySet: Canonical category nodes in this artifact's tree section
notes: This artifact is for product and data-model planning. It does not require the prototype to hardcode all fields immediately.
Character category describes the entity shape and field profile. Narrative function, ancestry, profession, faction rank, and moral alignment are flexible fields, not fixed character categories.
Ancestry is the preferred field name for humanoid race, species, people, or lineage concepts. It should accept creator-defined values and optional lore links.
Profession is the preferred field name for class, job, calling, trade, or adventuring role concepts. It should accept creator-defined values and optional faction or lore links.
Relationship-backed fields should become typed selectors when the editor supports typed character fields; plain text remains acceptable for the current local prototype.

classificationPrinciples.principle: Use character category for what kind of entity the record represents, and use fields for how the story uses that entity.
examples: A protagonist can be a humanoid person, construct, spirit, or collective character.
A villain, mentor, ruler, or witness is a narrative role field, not a category.

classificationPrinciples.principle: Ancestry, profession, culture, faith, title, and ability system should be flexible creator-authored values with optional links.
examples: The builder can suggest recently used values, but should not ship fixed ancestry or profession lists.
Sample data can use common-domain values, but the schema should not imply defaults.

classificationPrinciples.principle: Facts that connect records should be stored as relationships and displayed from both sides.
examples: A character residing in a city should make the city show resident characters.
A character employed by a guild should make the guild show members or staff.

classificationPrinciples.principle: Category-specific fields should guide the creator toward useful details while allowing sparse early drafts and unusual worlds.
examples: A construct may not have parents, but may have a maker, owner, directive, power source, and maintenance state.
A deity may not have a current residence, but may have a domain, manifestation places, worshipping factions, avatars, and myths.

relationshipTypes.label: same as
relationshipTypes.description: Two records represent the same identity, alias, incarnation, duplicate, mask, or version.

relationshipTypes.label: identity of
relationshipTypes.description: A public persona, secret identity, avatar, disguise, incarnation, or alternate form of another character.

relationshipTypes.label: ancestry described by
relationshipTypes.description: Links a creator-defined ancestry value to a lore note about biology, culture, origin, or inherited traits.

relationshipTypes.label: profession described by
relationshipTypes.description: Links a creator-defined profession value to a lore note about duties, class traditions, tools, training, or status.

relationshipTypes.label: originated from
relationshipTypes.description: Birthplace, creation site, awakening event, ancestral homeland, summoning source, or first appearance.

relationshipTypes.label: resides in
relationshipTypes.description: Current home, lair, station, court, temple, den, or usual base.

relationshipTypes.label: located at
relationshipTypes.description: Current known location when it differs from home or origin.

relationshipTypes.label: travels between
relationshipTypes.description: Regular route, migration range, patrol circuit, trade path, pilgrimage path, or hunting ground.

relationshipTypes.label: member of
relationshipTypes.description: Membership, citizenship, house, guild, crew, order, faith, school, military, or court attachment.

relationshipTypes.label: employed by
relationshipTypes.description: Employment, service, contract, appointment, patronage, or office.

relationshipTypes.label: leads
relationshipTypes.description: Formal or practical command over an organization, polity, settlement, crew, household, or follower.

relationshipTypes.label: rules
relationshipTypes.description: Sovereignty, stewardship, territorial dominion, court authority, divine patronage, or claimed rule.

relationshipTypes.label: serves
relationshipTypes.description: Service, oath, religious dedication, employment, duty, binding, or fealty.

relationshipTypes.label: served by
relationshipTypes.description: Followers, worshippers, attendants, servants, bound agents, caretakers, or communities that serve the character.

relationshipTypes.label: commands
relationshipTypes.description: Operational command over followers, troops, familiars, constructs, agents, or subordinates.

relationshipTypes.label: created by
relationshipTypes.description: Maker, inventor, parent process, ritual, accident, event, author, or divine act that created the character.

relationshipTypes.label: descended from
relationshipTypes.description: Genealogical descent, dynastic descent, spiritual descent, cloned lineage, or inherited mythic origin.

relationshipTypes.label: parent of
relationshipTypes.description: Biological, adoptive, spiritual, artificial, or creator-parent relationship.

relationshipTypes.label: child of
relationshipTypes.description: Biological, adoptive, spiritual, artificial, or creator-child relationship from the child record to parent records.

relationshipTypes.label: sibling of
relationshipTypes.description: Sibling, clutchmate, batchmate, oath-sibling, clone sibling, or equivalent peer lineage.

relationshipTypes.label: partner of
relationshipTypes.description: Romantic partner, spouse, bondmate, political marriage, bonded pair, or equivalent relationship.

relationshipTypes.label: mentor of
relationshipTypes.description: Teacher, trainer, patron, master, handler, predecessor, or guiding figure.

relationshipTypes.label: ally of
relationshipTypes.description: Cooperation, friendship, treaty, campaign alliance, shared agenda, or temporary partnership.

relationshipTypes.label: rival of
relationshipTypes.description: Competition, professional rivalry, succession contest, ideological rivalry, or personal foil.

relationshipTypes.label: enemy of
relationshipTypes.description: Hostile relationship, active opposition, vendetta, war, curse, predator-prey conflict, or sworn enmity.

relationshipTypes.label: owes obligation to
relationshipTypes.description: Debt, oath, curse, contract, taboo, vow, legal duty, divine command, or unresolved favor.

relationshipTypes.label: uses lore
relationshipTypes.description: Magic system, technology, martial tradition, ritual, language, artifact rule, medical condition, or doctrine.

relationshipTypes.label: bound to
relationshipTypes.description: Geas, curse, summoning bond, vessel, anchor, patron, contract, containment, oath, or metaphysical dependency.

relationshipTypes.label: participated in
relationshipTypes.description: Presence, participation, witness, casualty, author, victim, cause, or beneficiary in a timeline event.

relationshipTypes.label: caused event
relationshipTypes.description: Intentional or accidental cause of a timeline event.

relationshipTypes.label: changed by event
relationshipTypes.description: Event that transformed status, allegiance, body, powers, reputation, location, or motivation.

relationshipTypes.label: appears in lore
relationshipTypes.description: Myth, prophecy, law, song, chronicle, bestiary note, doctrine, rumor, or research record mentioning the character.

fieldCatalog.label: Character category
fieldCatalog.description: The entity-shape category used to choose relevant field groups.

fieldCatalog.label: Name
fieldCatalog.description: Primary display name for the character record.

fieldCatalog.label: Alternate names
fieldCatalog.description: Aliases, translated names, epithets, true names, secret names, designations, or public handles.

fieldCatalog.label: Summary
fieldCatalog.description: Short drafting summary used in lists and reference views.

fieldCatalog.label: Record status
fieldCatalog.description: Drafting status such as draft, canon, needs review, deprecated, or archived.

fieldCatalog.label: Tags
fieldCatalog.description: Loose labels for search, filtering, and draft organization.

fieldCatalog.label: Narrative role
fieldCatalog.description: Story function such as point-of-view figure, mentor, antagonist, witness, patron, foil, or background figure.

fieldCatalog.label: Current status
fieldCatalog.description: In-fiction condition such as alive, missing, exiled, dormant, cursed, transformed, ruling, hunted, or unknown.

fieldCatalog.label: Ancestry
fieldCatalog.description: Creator-defined humanoid race, species, people, heritage, lineage, or origin concept. Do not hardcode a default list.

fieldCatalog.label: Ancestry lore
fieldCatalog.description: Optional links to lore notes defining ancestry biology, culture, history, taboos, inherited traits, or social treatment.

fieldCatalog.label: Culture
fieldCatalog.description: Creator-defined cultural identity, upbringing, social tradition, diaspora, or adopted community.

fieldCatalog.label: Homeland
fieldCatalog.description: Ancestral, cultural, or emotional place of origin.

fieldCatalog.label: Origin
fieldCatalog.description: Birth, creation, awakening, summoning, manufacture, first appearance, or mythic source.

fieldCatalog.label: Age
fieldCatalog.description: Exact age, apparent age, age band, era, generation, or unknown age note.

fieldCatalog.label: Lifespan
fieldCatalog.description: Expected lifespan, immortality rule, decay cycle, service life, reincarnation cycle, or seasonal lifecycle.

fieldCatalog.label: Gender
fieldCatalog.description: Creator-defined gender or social gender note where relevant.

fieldCatalog.label: Pronouns
fieldCatalog.description: Pronouns for prose drafting and reference.

fieldCatalog.label: Languages
fieldCatalog.description: Languages, registers, telepathy modes, signal systems, machine protocols, or ritual speech.

fieldCatalog.label: Home
fieldCatalog.description: Home, lair, nest, residence, station, den, court, temple, grove, ship, or base.

fieldCatalog.label: Current location
fieldCatalog.description: Where the character is currently known to be.

fieldCatalog.label: Territory or range
fieldCatalog.description: Hunting range, patrol route, kingdom, divine domain, haunting ground, operating theater, migration route, or area of influence.

fieldCatalog.label: Profession
fieldCatalog.description: Creator-defined class, job, trade, calling, office, station, vocation, or specialist role. Do not hardcode a default list.

fieldCatalog.label: Profession lore
fieldCatalog.description: Optional links to lore notes defining training, duties, tools, class traditions, guild status, or social expectations.

fieldCatalog.label: Affiliations
fieldCatalog.description: Guilds, houses, faiths, armies, schools, crews, courts, orders, movements, or citizenship.

fieldCatalog.label: Rank or title
fieldCatalog.description: Formal rank, noble title, office, honorific, license, clergy rank, military grade, ritual status, or bestowed epithet.

fieldCatalog.label: Employer or patron
fieldCatalog.description: Employers, patrons, sponsors, owners, command structures, or appointing authorities.

fieldCatalog.label: Authority over
fieldCatalog.description: Followers, staff, troops, retainers, settlements, institutions, households, courts, territories, or agents.

fieldCatalog.label: Resources
fieldCatalog.description: Wealth, equipment, contacts, ships, sanctuaries, troops, legal powers, secrets, habitats, worship, or energy supply.

fieldCatalog.label: Skills
fieldCatalog.description: Practical skills, training, knowledge domains, social skills, martial forms, survival methods, or technical capabilities.

fieldCatalog.label: Abilities
fieldCatalog.description: Magic, powers, technologies, mutations, senses, transformations, immunities, talents, or extraordinary methods.

fieldCatalog.label: Limitations
fieldCatalog.description: Weaknesses, costs, taboos, vulnerabilities, failure modes, dependencies, maintenance limits, curses, or social constraints.

fieldCatalog.label: Appearance
fieldCatalog.description: Build, height, coloration, clothing, silhouette, movement, voice, scent, markings, or first visual impression.

fieldCatalog.label: Distinguishing features
fieldCatalog.description: Scars, mutations, signature tools, heraldry, aura, prosthetics, voice, behavior, markings, or identifying details.

fieldCatalog.label: Condition
fieldCatalog.description: Health, damage, corruption, enchantment, maintenance state, hunger, spiritual stability, sanity, or transformation status.

fieldCatalog.label: Personality
fieldCatalog.description: Temperament, habits, social style, values, humor, self-image, speech pattern, and decision tendencies.

fieldCatalog.label: Motivations
fieldCatalog.description: Wants, needs, fears, promises, grudges, ambitions, duties, survival drives, hungers, or divine mandates.

fieldCatalog.label: Current goal
fieldCatalog.description: Immediate objective that can drive scenes, conflicts, or timeline events.

fieldCatalog.label: Secrets
fieldCatalog.description: Hidden identity, concealed history, private motive, forbidden knowledge, secret bond, weakness, or unrevealed fact.

fieldCatalog.label: Character arc
fieldCatalog.description: Expected change, unresolved contradiction, fall, recovery, transformation, or static function.

fieldCatalog.label: Parents or creators
fieldCatalog.description: Parents, creators treated as family, adoptive parents, spiritual parents, or origin figures.

fieldCatalog.label: Siblings or peers
fieldCatalog.description: Siblings, clutchmates, batchmates, peer clones, oath-siblings, or equivalent close peers.

fieldCatalog.label: Partners
fieldCatalog.description: Spouses, romantic partners, bondmates, political partners, bonded pairs, or equivalent.

fieldCatalog.label: Children or creations
fieldCatalog.description: Children, creations treated as children, successors, descendants, offspring, or derived beings.

fieldCatalog.label: Mentors
fieldCatalog.description: Teachers, trainers, guides, handlers, patrons, old masters, predecessors, or origin guides.

fieldCatalog.label: Allies
fieldCatalog.description: Friends, allies, collaborators, party members, coalition partners, or temporary supporters.

fieldCatalog.label: Rivals
fieldCatalog.description: Foils, professional competitors, succession rivals, ideological opponents, or personal rivals.

fieldCatalog.label: Enemies
fieldCatalog.description: Opponents, hunters, prey, sworn enemies, hostile factions, or active threats.

fieldCatalog.label: Obligations
fieldCatalog.description: Debts, vows, contracts, taboos, curses, legal duties, bonds, services owed, or favors owed.

fieldCatalog.label: Notable events
fieldCatalog.description: Events involving, changing, creating, wounding, exiling, appointing, transforming, or killing the character.

fieldCatalog.label: Related lore
fieldCatalog.description: Prophecies, myths, laws, bestiary entries, rituals, artifacts, diseases, technologies, customs, or research notes.

fieldCatalog.label: Habitat
fieldCatalog.description: Natural habitat, lair, range, nest, plane, biome, containment site, or preferred environment.

fieldCatalog.label: Diet or fuel
fieldCatalog.description: Food, prey, energy source, sunlight, blood, prayers, magic, batteries, memory, dreams, or maintenance input.

fieldCatalog.label: Behavior
fieldCatalog.description: Instincts, social behavior, territoriality, hunting pattern, routines, command responses, or manifestation pattern.

fieldCatalog.label: Threat level
fieldCatalog.description: Creator-defined danger, political risk, combat risk, ecological risk, social risk, or containment priority.

fieldCatalog.label: Handler or keeper
fieldCatalog.description: Handler, keeper, trainer, summoner, owner, pilot, custodian, priest, or caretaker.

fieldCatalog.label: Maker
fieldCatalog.description: Inventor, creator, manufacturer, summoner, ritual source, divine maker, parent event, or design lineage.

fieldCatalog.label: Model or design
fieldCatalog.description: Construct model, spell form, automaton class, manufactured line, prototype name, vessel type, or ritual design.

fieldCatalog.label: Directive
fieldCatalog.description: Primary instruction, operating law, geas, programming, bound service, mission, purpose, or original design goal.

fieldCatalog.label: Autonomy
fieldCatalog.description: Free will, obedience constraints, self-awareness, legal personhood, command hierarchy, or sentience state.

fieldCatalog.label: Maintenance
fieldCatalog.description: Repair needs, ritual upkeep, spare parts, decay, service intervals, memory resets, or sustaining conditions.

fieldCatalog.label: Domain
fieldCatalog.description: Divine portfolio, spirit concern, haunting theme, conceptual jurisdiction, natural force, or metaphysical territory.

fieldCatalog.label: Anchor
fieldCatalog.description: Object, place, event, person, oath, body, shrine, grave, ritual, vessel, or condition that binds the character.

fieldCatalog.label: Manifestation
fieldCatalog.description: How an immaterial, divine, undead, or shapeshifting character appears and interacts with the world.

fieldCatalog.label: Worshippers or cult
fieldCatalog.description: Followers, cults, priesthoods, temples, patron communities, saints, devotees, or bargain networks.

fieldCatalog.label: Forms
fieldCatalog.description: Alternate bodies, masks, avatars, phases, seasonal shapes, false identities, or transformed states.

fieldCatalog.label: Transformation rules
fieldCatalog.description: Triggers, costs, limits, recognition clues, control rules, reversibility, and social consequences of changing form.

fieldCatalog.label: Members
fieldCatalog.description: Constituent bodies, voices, linked minds, swarm members, family line representatives, or composite parts.

fieldCatalog.label: Spokespersons
fieldCatalog.description: Individuals who speak for, embody, host, or represent a collective character.

fieldCatalog.label: Shared mind
fieldCatalog.description: How a collective identity coordinates memory, intent, disagreement, separation, and decision-making.

fieldCatalog.label: Succession
fieldCatalog.description: Predecessors, successors, incarnations, dynastic descent, office transfer, lineage crisis, or future heir.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Record basics
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields every character record needs for list display, search, and drafting state.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Category and role
commonFieldProfiles.description: Separates entity shape from story function and current in-fiction state.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Identity and origin
commonFieldProfiles.description: Personal, cultural, biological, created, or mythic identity fields.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Place connections
commonFieldProfiles.description: Location fields that should link to place records and display inverse character lists on places.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Profession and power
commonFieldProfiles.description: Work, office, training, power, class-like concepts, and institutional relationships.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Presentation
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields for recognizing and writing the character in scenes.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Story engine
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields that make the character useful for plot, conflict, and revision.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Relationship graph
commonFieldProfiles.description: Typed links to other records. These should be graph edges rather than duplicated prose when possible.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Story links
commonFieldProfiles.description: Common non-family relationship links for categories where biological or social-family fields are not usually primary.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Creature profile
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields for animals, familiars, monsters, and creature-like characters.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Construct profile
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields for artificial, built, programmed, summoned, or manufactured characters.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Supernatural profile
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields for spirits, undead, divine figures, planar beings, and metaphysical characters.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Shapeshifter profile
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields for characters with multiple bodies, identities, forms, phases, or avatars.

commonFieldProfiles.label: Collective profile
commonFieldProfiles.description: Fields for hive minds, swarms, dynastic personas, shared bodies, or composite identities treated as a character.

tree.label: Character
tree.description: Named figures, beings, creatures, identities, and character-like entities in the world.

children.label: Individual sapient characters
children.description: Characters with person-like agency, identity, social relationships, and decision-making.

children.label: Humanoid person
children.description: A person-like character whose ancestry, culture, profession, affiliations, relationships, and places are the central drafting concerns.
useWhen: The character participates in society as a person.
Ancestry, culture, profession, family, affiliations, and residence are useful fields.
The setting might call the ancestry race, species, people, lineage, or heritage, but the field should be named ancestry.
avoidWhen: The record is mainly a political organization or army; use faction.
The record is mainly a species overview, custom, or biology note; use lore.

children.label: Nonhumanoid sapient
children.description: An intelligent individual with agency but a non-person-default body, culture, communication mode, lifecycle, or habitat.
useWhen: The character makes choices, forms relationships, and can hold goals or obligations.
Biology, habitat, communication, and social treatment matter alongside personality and profession.
avoidWhen: The record is a generic creature type; use lore.
The record is an animal without person-like agency; use animal or creature.

children.label: Shapeshifter or variant identity
children.description: A character whose forms, masks, avatars, public identities, phases, or transformed states are central to the record.
useWhen: A single character needs linked forms or identities.
Recognition, transformation rules, costs, and identity ambiguity matter.
avoidWhen: A public alias is only a name; use alternate names on the base category.
Each identity has independent relationships and should be drafted separately; use linked character records with same-as or identity-of.
linkFocus: forms to character when forms are record-worthy
secrets to prose until secret visibility exists

children.label: Biological creature characters
children.description: Named animals, beasts, familiars, mounts, monsters, and other living characters where ecology and behavior are important.

children.label: Animal or companion
children.description: A named animal, mount, familiar, pet, working animal, or companion where handler bonds, training, behavior, and habitat matter.
useWhen: The record is a specific named creature rather than a species note.
A handler, rider, owner, bonded character, or home place is important.
avoidWhen: The record is a species, ecology article, or bestiary entry; use lore.
The character has full person-like social agency; consider nonhumanoid sapient.

children.label: Monster or threat
children.description: A named dangerous creature or adversarial being where threat, range, vulnerabilities, casualties, and related lore are important.
useWhen: The character is a specific named threat.
Containment, hunting grounds, weaknesses, victims, and events matter.
avoidWhen: The record is a general creature taxonomy; use lore.
The threat is an organized group; use faction.

children.label: Artificial or made characters
children.description: Constructs, machines, animated objects, built bodies, artificial intelligences, and manufactured or ritual-made beings.

children.label: Construct or automaton
children.description: A built, animated, manufactured, or programmed character with a maker, design, directive, autonomy state, and maintenance needs.
useWhen: The character was built, programmed, animated, assembled, cloned, or ritually made.
Maker, owner, directive, autonomy, power source, and repair are useful fields.
avoidWhen: The record is an item without agency; use lore.
The record is a faction of machines; use faction.
linkFocus: maker to character, faction, lore, or timeline
employerOrPatron to owner or commissioning body
maintenance to lore when rules are reusable

children.label: Artificial intelligence or disembodied mind
children.description: A created mind, machine intelligence, bound memory, archive persona, spell intelligence, or disembodied consciousness.
useWhen: The character's body may be distributed, replaceable, virtual, or absent.
Hosting places, vessels, directives, autonomy, memory, and access control are important.
avoidWhen: The record is a software system or magical rule without character agency; use lore.
linkFocus: anchor to place, lore, or host character
createdBy to maker or creation event
usesLore to technology or magic system

children.label: Supernatural or otherworldly characters
children.description: Spirits, undead, deities, avatars, planar beings, cosmic entities, and characters defined by metaphysical rules.

children.label: Spirit or undead
children.description: A ghost, revenant, ancestral spirit, elemental spirit, bound soul, lingering dead, or other nonliving character with anchor and manifestation rules.
useWhen: The character's existence depends on a death, binding, unfinished duty, haunting, vessel, or metaphysical condition.
Anchors, banes, manifestation rules, and related events are important.
avoidWhen: The record is a general afterlife rule; use lore.
The record is a worshipped cosmic power; consider deity or cosmic entity.
linkFocus: origin to death or binding event
anchor to place, character, lore, or event
notableEvents to death, curse, or release event

children.label: Deity or divine figure
children.description: A god, saint, demigod, patron power, divine ancestor, idol-being, or worshipped figure with domains, worshippers, avatars, and myths.
useWhen: Worship, portfolios, temples, avatars, divine obligations, and mythic events matter.
The character can be linked to factions, places, and lore through worship and doctrine.
avoidWhen: The record is only a religion or church; use faction.
The record is only a theology note; use lore.
linkFocus: worshippers to factions, places, or characters
territory to temples, holy places, or realms

children.label: Cosmic or planar entity
children.description: A vast, alien, planar, conceptual, stellar, or reality-scale being whose scale and rules may not fit normal bodies or societies.
useWhen: The character has agency but may also be a place-like, lore-like, or event-like force.
Domain, manifestation, scale, anchors, affected places, and cults matter.
avoidWhen: The record is a realm or dimension without character agency; use place.
The record is a cosmology rule; use lore.
linkFocus: territory to realms, planes, stars, or affected places
notableEvents to awakenings, omens, incursions, or banishments

children.label: Composite or collective characters
children.description: Characters treated as one narrative identity despite being made of multiple bodies, voices, iterations, heirs, or hosts.

children.label: Collective character
children.description: A hive mind, swarm identity, chorus, linked council, possessed host-chain, living city voice, or group treated as a single character.
useWhen: The story treats the collective as one acting identity.
Member links, spokespersons, shared memory, disagreement, and severed members matter.
avoidWhen: The record is primarily an organization with membership and goals; use faction.
The record is a population or ancestry overview; use lore.
linkFocus: notableEvents to schisms, assimilations, or severance

children.label: Lineage or succession persona
children.description: A mantle, inherited identity, dynastic persona, reincarnation chain, office-name, or legacy treated as a character across holders.
useWhen: Different people hold the same meaningful identity across time.
Predecessors, successors, masks, legitimacy, and transfer events matter.
avoidWhen: The lineage is mainly a family organization or house; use faction.
Each holder has no shared identity; use relationships between normal character records.
linkFocus: succession to predecessor and successor characters
notableEvents to investiture, death, abdication, or revelation
rankOrTitle to office lore or faction
forms to current holder or public identity

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Ancestry is a custom value. Reused ancestry definitions should be represented by lore links, not a fixed enum.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: Writers often need inherited traits, social treatment, origin, language, and lifecycle near ancestry decisions.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Profession is a custom value. Reused profession definitions should be represented by lore links and optional faction links.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: Class-like concepts usually imply training, social status, tools, employers, guilds, duties, and constraints.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Home should use resides-in. Current location should use located-at. Origin should use originated-from.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: A character can be from one place, live in another, and currently travel through a third.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Titles should link to a faction, place, or lore note when they grant authority or meaning.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: A rank without its institution, jurisdiction, or duties is hard to query or display usefully.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Reusable magic, technology, training, and biology rules should live in lore; per-character expression can remain text.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: World consistency depends on pairing power descriptions with costs, constraints, sources, and exceptions.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Constructs can still use ancestry or parents if the fiction supports it, but maker and design are usually higher-signal.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: Artificial beings need creation and operation fields more than biological genealogy.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Use anchor for metaphysical dependency. Use homePlace only when the spirit also has a meaningful residence or haunting place.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: The binding object, place, person, or event is often the core query for supernatural characters.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Use faction instead when the group is primarily an organization rather than one acting identity.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: Collective characters require clear links between the whole identity and its constituent characters.

fieldRelationshipRules.dataRule: Major changes such as death, exile, transformation, appointment, release, and disappearance should link to timeline events.
fieldRelationshipRules.reason: The timeline should explain why the current character sheet is true.

suggestedMapping.notes: Current role values may mix story role, job, title, and social function.
Current home values may be current residence, birthplace, base, or cultural origin.
Current affiliation values should become faction links when possible.
Current status notes mix in-fiction state, health, task, and plot situation.

softValidation: Warn, do not block, when a category uses unusual fields.
Allow custom ancestry and profession values without requiring lore records.
Suggest converting repeated free-text place, faction, lore, and character names into links.
Preserve free-text notes for ambiguity, secrets, and half-formed drafts.

implementationRecommendations: Start with a visible character category selector and keep the existing flexible fields storage.
Add ancestry and profession as custom text fields with autocomplete from values already used in the current workspace, not from a bundled default list.
Add optional lore links beside ancestry and profession so reusable rules can be centralized without forcing early drafts to create lore notes.
Promote home, affiliation, and notable relationships to relationship-backed selectors before attempting to model every field as structured data.
Display inverse links on related records: residents on places, members on factions, participants on timeline events, and mentioned characters on lore.
Use category field profiles to decide which fields are shown by default and which are tucked into an advanced/details area.
Keep narrative role, current status, secrets, and arc as draft-friendly text fields even after high-value graph links become typed.
Prefer warnings and suggestions over constraints. Fantasy and speculative fiction need deliberate exceptions.

allowed: Sample characters may use common-domain ancestry examples such as human, elf, dwarf, fae, giant, or alien when they fit the sample world.
Sample characters may use common-domain profession examples such as surveyor, scholar, druid, soldier, courier, miner, or priest.
Samples should demonstrate custom values and links without implying that those values are built-in.
avoid: Do not ship fixed ancestry or profession option lists as part of the codex builder.
Do not use ancestry where culture, nationality, faction membership, or profession is the more precise field.
Do not encode copyrighted species, classes, organizations, or setting-specific terms as defaults.
